title: Tangy Tender Festive Saudia Cauliflower Stir-fry
description: A tangy and festive cauliflower dish infused with Middle Eastern flavors, perfect for a savory side or light meal.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: For sautéing vegetables and adding flavor to the cauliflower

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: For its umami taste and natural acidity

ingredients.name: Red Pepper Flakes
ingredients.notes: To give it a slight heat, optional

instructions: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Toss the cauliflower florets with olive oil until coated. Place them on the prepared baking sheet and roast for about 20 minutes, flipping halfway through.
Meanwhile, in a small bowl, mix together tomato paste, soy sauce, white wine vinegar, oregano, red pepper flakes (if using), salt, and black pepper to create your tangy marinade. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add finely chopped onion and sauté for about 5 minutes until translucent.
Add garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Pour in the reserved marinade from step 3, stir well, then add roasted cauliflower florets to the pan.
Stir everything together thoroughly. Cover with a lid if you have one and let it cook for an additional 5-10 minutes until the cauliflower is tender and nicely coated with the sauce.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course depending on your preference.
